behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not only local counting logic; correctness depends on how git reports renames across module boundaries (CLI -> subprocess git -> parser). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename detection is git behavior and flag/threshold dependent; mocked output can hide a real bug where the tool invokes git without rename detection and would emit add+delete. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, real subprocess/git semantics are required -> System; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is about real repo outcomes ("Renamed files ...") not just parsing fixtures. Rejected Agentic because scope is deterministic plumbing: "A Python CLI tool reads a git repository ... outputs a structured report" and there is no model output quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it via git mv, run the CLI, and assert report has 1 rename and 0 add/0 delete for that change; verify via real stdout parsing.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at boundary between git subprocess output encoding and parser when binary content is present. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real git binary diff behavior (and byte/encoding edge cases) is exactly what can crash; fixture strings often miss it. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, needs real subprocess and filesystem binary file -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly targets real "Diffs that include binary files". Rejected Agentic because the behavior is deterministic CLI robustness/counting, and text excludes AI/model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add and modify a real binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI against that diff range, assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in the expected category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unborn-HEAD behavior is produced by real git command exit/status interaction, not just local logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked git responses can hide failures around non-zero exit codes/message handling from subprocess calls. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, requires real git repo state and subprocess behavior -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior references actual repo lifecycle state ("repository that has no commits yet"). Rejected Agentic because this is deterministic error-handling plumbing with no model output content dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new temp git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, assert successful exit and an empty structured report on stdout (not an exception or error report).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates end-to-end mapping from real repository history through git commands into final CLI report counts. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: if git command selection/range semantics are wrong, mocked outputs can still pass while real histories fail. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, relies on real subprocess + repo history semantics -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text anchors to "actual change history of the repository being analyzed," which disqualifies fixture-only wiring as sufficient. Rejected Agentic because there is no AI/ML output quality in scope.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp git repo with a scripted sequence of add/modify/delete/rename commits with known expected totals, run CLI over the target history window, and assert category counts exactly match expected real-history totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure occurs when comparing real index vs working-tree states across git invocations. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: staged/unstaged distinctions are git+filesystem semantics; mocks can miss subtle command/flag mismatches. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, needs real git subprocess and filesystem/index behavior -> System; (b)(c)(d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly compares real repository states ("staged or unstaged"). Rejected Agentic because behavior is deterministic CLI diff-source equivalence, with no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent content changes once as staged and once as unstaged in controlled runs, execute CLI for each scenario, and assert stdout report bytes are identical.
